items.title: Discrete PMF vs continuous PDF
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  rv["Random variable X"]
  rv --> discrete["Discrete: PMF P(X=x)"]
  rv --> continuous["Continuous: PDF f(x)"]
  discrete --> sum1["Non-negative masses sum to 1"]
  continuous --> int1["Integral of f over R equals 1"]
items.caption: PMF values are probabilities at points; PDF values are densities—probabilities come from integrating over intervals.

items.title: Bernoulli to Binomial
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  b1["Bernoulli trial 1"] --> sumNode["sum successes"]
  b2["Bernoulli trial 2"] --> sumNode
  bn["Bernoulli trial n"] --> sumNode
  sumNode --> binom["Binomial(n, p) count"]
items.caption: Binomial counts how many successes occur in n independent Bernoulli(p) trials.

items.title: ML output layer to distribution
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  logits["model logits"] --> softmax["softmax / sigmoid"]
  softmax --> cat["Categorical or Bernoulli pi"]
  cat --> ce["cross-entropy loss"]
  features["features per class"] --> gauss["Gaussian PDF in GNB"]
  gauss --> post["class posterior"]
items.caption: Classification heads parameterize Categorical or Bernoulli laws; Gaussian NB uses Normal densities per feature and class.
